description_long: This is a small, simple cabin with a low, wide profile and a single, dark gabled roof. The structure is compact, with a rectangular base and a roof that overhangs slightly on all sides. The exterior appears to be finished in a light, neutral color, possibly painted wood or siding, with a darker roof that contrasts sharply with the walls. There are no visible windows or doors on the sides shown, giving the cabin a very minimal and utilitarian appearance. The roof is the most prominent feature, with a clear gable shape and a uniform surface. The overall design suggests a basic, functional shelter, likely intended for single occupancy or as a small outbuilding.
description: A compact, rectangular cabin with a dark gabled roof and light-colored walls, featuring a minimal, windowless exterior. The structure is simple and utilitarian, with a low profile and no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: From this view, the gabled roof is clearly visible, with the peak running horizontally and the light-colored walls beneath it.
description_view_1: This angle shows the side of the cabin, emphasizing the overhanging roof and the solid, windowless wall.
description_view_2: The gable end of the roof is prominent here, with the dark roof contrasting against the light wall below.
description_view_3: This view mirrors view 1, showing the opposite side of the cabin with the same overhanging roof and plain wall.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with moderate overhangs and a uniform, dark surface
attribute_window_arrangement: No windows are visible on any side of the cabin; the exterior appears completely windowless from all shown angles.
attribute_door_placement: No exterior doors are visible in the provided views; the entrance may be on an unseen side.
